title: peschiera benthamiana var. myriantha
intro: Peschiera benthamiana var. myriantha, also known as Myriantha, is a plant species in the family Violaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The health benefits of Myriantha include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Myriantha is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a cream or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
